Estimates

Stipends, living costs, competitiveness and effort — figures are estimates; confirm on official pages before relying on them.

Competitiveness

SchemeOddsDifficultyNotes
Clarendon Scholarship (Oxford)~7% historically (1,800 applicants for 124 awards, 2009-10 data); now ~200+ awards/year but current applicant pool size not publishedVery high — but note ALL Oxford grad applicants meeting deadline are auto-considered, no separate application, so 'applicant pool' = entire Oxford grad applicant pool for that round, not a self-selected subset[Estimate, dated] Only concrete ratio found is historical (2009-10): 1,800 apps / 124 awards = ~7%. Current scale is larger (200+/year per https://www.ox.ac.uk/clarendon, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clarendon_Fund) but current applicant denominator not disclosed publicly. Treat ~7% as a rough anchor, likely stale given scholarship count has grown. Source: https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/fees-and-funding/funding/clarendon, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clarendon_Fund
Gates Cambridge Scholarship~1.5-3% (some sources cite as low as 0.5-1.3%); ~80-90 scholars/year selected from several thousand applicants, ~2/3 go to PhD studentsExtremely high — one of the most competitive scholarships globally[Found, current] Multiple sources converge: acceptance rate 1.5-3% typical, with low end 0.5-1.3% in some cited years. ~80 awards/year (25 US round + 55 international round), ~2/3 PhD. Source: https://www.gatescambridge.org/apply/timeline/, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gates_Cambridge_Scholarship, https://scholarships360.org/scholarships/gates-cambridge-scholarship/
Harding Distinguished Postgraduate Scholars Programme (Cambridge)Unknown/not published — award count itself is inconsistent across sources (25-40/year), no applicant denominator foundPresumed very high given comparable UK full-cost scholarships, but cannot be quantified from available data[Not found — gap] Searches found conflicting award-count figures (~40/year per newer program materials vs ~25 PhD/year per older sources) and NO applicant total or acceptance rate anywhere in public sources. Recommend direct email to HardingScholarship@admin.cam.ac.uk if a real number is needed. Source: https://www.hardingscholars.fund.cam.ac.uk/, https://www.globalsouthopportunities.com/2025/11/04/harding/
Knight-Hennessy Scholars (Stanford)~0.9-1.7% — 2026 cohort: 87 selected from 9,223 total applications (5,144 eligible); 2025 cohort acceptance rate reported at 0.9%Extremely high — among the most selective graduate scholarships worldwide, explicitly cross-disciplinary/leadership-focused not just academic merit[Found, current, high confidence] 2026 cohort: 9,223 applications (+7.6% YoY from 8,570 for 2025 cohort), 5,144 eligible, 87 selected → ~1.7% of eligible or ~0.9% of total. 2025 cohort rate cited directly as 0.9%. Source: https://knight-hennessy.stanford.edu/admission, https://knight-hennessy.stanford.edu/news/now-open-khs-application-2026-cohort, https://menlocoaching.com/top-mba-programs/stanford-gsb/knight-hennessy-scholars/acceptance-rate/
Imperial President's PhD ScholarshipNot published — 50 scholarships/year confirmed but no applicant total found in any year searched (2020-2025)Presumed very high (fully-funded, all-fields, prestige scholarship at a top-10 global university) but cannot be quantified[Not found — gap] Consistent across all searches: Imperial does not publish applicant numbers or acceptance rate for this scheme. Only the award count (50/year) is public. Would need direct contact with student.funding@imperial.ac.uk for real figures — do not fabricate a percentage here. Source: https://www.imperial.ac.uk/study/fees-and-funding/postgraduate-doctoral/grants-scholarships/presidents-phd/
EMBL International PhD Programme~3% (single alumnus-sourced estimate, unverified against an official EMBL statistic); 2 recruitment rounds/year, positions limited relative to 'high number of strong applications' per EMBL's own descriptionVery high — but note EMBL explicitly screens for research fit/lab availability alongside merit, somewhat like ETH's supervisor-first model[Estimate, low confidence] The ~3% figure comes from a single Quora response citing personal experience as an EIPP graduate, not an official EMBL statistic — flag as weak sourcing. EMBL's own site only says application volume is 'high' without a number. Source: https://www.quora.com/How-is-EMBL-PhD-programme (weak), https://www.embl.org/about/info/embl-international-phd-programme/overview/
Wellcome Sanger Institute PhD Programme~37% offer rate for the Sanger-affiliated Cambridge 'PhD in Biological Science' track (2023/24 entry data); 4-year PhD Programme has ~12 funded studentships/yearHigh but notably less brutal than the other schemes on this list — this is the one place where a published, dated offer rate exists and it is far more favorable (~1 in 3) than Gates/Knight-Hennessy/EMBL[Found, but dated to 2023/24 cycle] 37% offer rate specifically for the Cambridge-administered 'PhD in Biological Science (Sanger Institute)' course code, not necessarily identical to the standalone 4-year PhD Programme's own applicant pool — treat as directionally useful, not exact. 12 studentships/year confirmed for 2026 intake. Source: https://www.admissionreport.com/university-of-cambridge/phd-biological-science/sanger-institute, https://www.sanger.ac.uk/about/study/phd-programmes/4-year-phd-programme/
Top-5 US CS PhD admission (Stanford/MIT/CMU/Berkeley/CMU-tier)~3-5% overall PhD-level (Berkeley CS PhD ~3.4% cited); Stanford's 16% figure found is almost certainly conflated with Master's/other program data, not pure PhD — treat with suspicion; ML/AI subfield specifically is the most oversubscribed sub-area within CS, likely pushing effective odds toward the low single digits for ML/AI-focused applicantsExtremely high, particularly for ML/AI which is the most saturated CS PhD subfield nationally; CMU noted as not publishing a clean program-specific PhD rate at all[Mixed confidence — one figure likely wrong] Berkeley CS PhD ~3.4% is a plausible PhD-specific figure. Stanford's commonly-cited 16% almost certainly blends non-PhD (MS/other) admits and should NOT be read as the PhD rate — no source separated it cleanly. MIT and CMU: no clean PhD-only percentage found in any source; CMU explicitly flagged as not disclosing one. For applicant's ML/computational-bio-adjacent profile, expect real competition at the harder end of this range, not the softer Stanford-looking number. Source: https://orieladmissions.com/best-colleges-for-computer-science/, https://admit-lab.com/blog/carnegie-mellon-computer-science-phd-acceptance-rate/, https://timdettmers.com/2018/11/26/phd-applications/
ETH Zurich / EPFL Doctoral ProgramsETH: ~10-15% cited, but this figure is structurally misleading (see difficulty note); EPFL: no percentage found anywhereStructurally different from UK/US competitive-pool model — at ETH/EPFL a doctorate requires securing a specific professor's sponsorship/funded position FIRST, then formal admission follows almost automatically; so the real bottleneck is landing a supervisor/position (informal, decentralized, not centrally tracked), not clearing a central admissions committee[Found but caveated — important structural note for applicant] The 10-15% figure for ETH conflates two different funnels: getting a professor to take you on (the real, unmeasured bottleneck) vs formal doctoral registration (near-automatic once you have a position and a completed Master's). No centralized applicant/acceptance data exists because there is no single admissions round. EPFL: same decentralized model, explicitly confirmed no acceptance-rate statistic exists. Practical implication: competitiveness is about the specific lab/PI, not an institution-wide rate. Source: https://ethz.ch/en/doctorate/registration-admission/zulassungsbedingungen.html, https://www.epfl.ch/education/admission/admission-2/phd-admission-criteria-and-application/

Stipends

Scheme / positionAmountNotes
Clarendon Scholarship (Oxford)£15,009/year (2025-26, full-time)FOUND. Covers fees in full plus this living-cost grant. Part-time DPhil rate: at least £2,502/year. Source: University of Oxford, ox.ac.uk/clarendon/information-for-applicants/full-time
Gates Cambridge Scholarship£22,050/year (2025-26, 12 months, single student)FOUND. Pro-rated for courses under 12 months; PhD award runs up to 4 years. Source: gatescambridge.org/programme/the-scholarship/
Cambridge Trust Scholarship (standard, e.g. Cambridge International Scholarship)£21,000/year (2025-26)FOUND, but slightly less certain than the others — official cambridgetrust.org pages returned empty on direct fetch, so this rests on secondary-source search results that consistently cite £21,000 for 2025-26 and clearly distinguish it from the higher Gates Cambridge rate (£22,050). Also covers University Composition Fee, return airfare, visa costs, and IHS. Treat as [Likely] rather than fully confirmed against the primary page.
Harding Distinguished Postgraduate Scholars Programme (Cambridge)£21,360/year maintenance (2025-26)FOUND. Plus University Composition Fee and a separate £6,000 total personal research allowance over the PhD. Source: hardingscholars.fund.cam.ac.uk / student-funding.cam.ac.uk
Imperial College President's PhD Scholarship£25,980/year (2025/26 rate)FOUND after correcting an initial mix-up: many aggregator pages quote £26,500, but that is confirmed as the 2026-27 rate, not 2025-26. £25,980 (2025/26) confirmed via scholarshipbob.com quoting the official phrasing 'A stipend of £25,980 (2025/26 rate) per annum'; consistent with Imperial's official presidents-phd page structure. Also includes full tuition fees + £2,000/year consumables fund for first 3 years.
UKRI minimum doctoral stipend (UK-wide)£20,780/year standard; £22,780/year London weighting (effective 1 Oct 2025, for 2025/26)FOUND. This was an 8% increase from 2024-25, described by UKRI as the largest real-terms rise since 2003, intended to track take-home national living wage. Source: ukri.org/news/ukri-is-increasing-phd-stipends-and-improving-student-support/
Harvard University — GSAS/SEAS PhD stipend (covers CS PhD, which is administered jointly by SEAS and GSAS)$50,460/year (~$4,205/month, 12-month, before tax) for AY2025-26CONFIRMED, current figure. Source: Harvard SEAS-GC stipend page (gc.seas.harvard.edu/stipend) states $4,205/month cost-of-living stipend plus $1,040 transportation/dental subsidy for AY25-26; consistent with Harvard Crimson (Dec 2023) reporting GSAS raised the PhD stipend floor to $50,000 starting July 2024, with subsequent standard COLA increases. All Harvard PhD admits (incl. CS/bioinformatics via SEAS or the Bioinformatics and Integrative Genomics / BBS programs) receive full funding at this rate or above.
Stanford University — university-wide minimum PhD stipend/salary (applies to CS PhD via GFS min-salary table; CS PhD funded via RA/TA/fellowship at or above this floor)ESTIMATE: approx. $56,000-$57,500/year (~$4,600-$4,800/month) for AY2025-26ESTIMATE, moderate confidence — the official GFS minimum salary table (gfs.stanford.edu/salary) returned HTTP 403 and could not be fetched directly, so the CS-specific exact figure is not independently confirmed here. Estimate is based on Stanford Biosciences PhD stipend of $56,348/year ($14,087/quarter) for 2025-26 (biosciences.stanford.edu), which tracks the university-wide GFS minimum that CS PhD funding is pegged to; Stanford GSB PhD (a different, business-school-specific stipend) is $57,480/year for 2025-26 as an upper reference point. An older/stale web result cited $3,500-4,500/month, which appears outdated and likely predates recent GFS increases — treat that lower figure as unreliable. Recommend verifying exact CS figure directly at cs.stanford.edu/phd-program-overview/funding or gfs.stanford.edu/salary once accessible.
MIT — EECS PhD stipend (RA/TA/Fellowship, applies to CS PhD)CONFIRMED: approx. $55,800-$56,300/year (~$4,650-$4,695/month) for AY2025-26CONFIRMED. Source: MIT EECS Funding Guide for Graduate Students AY25-26 (eecs.mit.edu, PDF: 'Funding-Guide-for-Graduate-Students-in-EECS-AY25-26.pdf') and MIT OGE stipend rates page (oge.mit.edu/graduate-admissions/costs-funding/stipend-rates). TA appointments: $4,695/month; general/RA-fellowship standard stipend cited around $4,654/month (~$55,848/year). Full package also includes tuition and a health insurance value of ~$3,603/year on top of stipend.
Caltech — university-wide PhD stipend (applies to CS/CMS PhD)CONFIRMED: $48,244/year ($4,020/month) effective Sept 1, 2025; increasing to $50,000/year ($4,166/month) effective Sept 1, 2026CONFIRMED, current figure with forward increase already scheduled. Source: Caltech Graduate Studies Office Estimated Budget page (gradoffice.caltech.edu/financialsupport/budget). This is the university-wide minimum PhD stipend that CS (CMS department) PhD students are funded at.
Princeton University — Graduate School PhD stipend, Natural Sciences & Engineering division (applies to CS PhD; first-year is University First-Year Fellowship, later years typically AI/AR assistantships at same or higher rate)ESTIMATE: approx. $46,000-$48,000/year for AY2025-26ESTIMATE, lower confidence — Princeton's official rate table (gsadminportal.princeton.edu funding policies page and gradschool.princeton.edu funding-calendar page) returned HTTP 403/blocked and the exact current dollar figure could not be directly confirmed. Estimate derived by compounding known historical rates (2022: $40,000/year for Natural Sciences & Engineering University First-Year Fellowship, 10-month) forward using Princeton's announced increases (4.25% for AY2024-25, then 3.2% average increase for AY2025-26 per princeton.edu news and graddiversity.princeton.edu). Recommend confirming exact figure at cs.princeton.edu/grad/funding or gradschool.princeton.edu/financial-support/financial-support-model/phd-funding-natural-sciences-engineering directly, as these pages exist but were inaccessible to automated fetch during this search.
ETH Zurich — Doctoral student (PhD), standard rate, gross annual salary by year, 100% positionCHF 61,300/year (Year 1) rising to CHF 64,200/year (Year 4+), i.e. approx. CHF 5,108–5,350 gross/month (paid over 12 months; ETH uses 12 monthly payments, not 13). Est. net after Swiss social security (AHV/IV/EO ~5.3%, ALV ~1.1%, pension fund, and cantonal/federal withholding tax for foreign nationals without C permit, roughly 12-17% total for a single person in Zurich) ≈ CHF 4,300-4,600/month net (ESTIMATE, not from a payslip).Effective 1 Jan 2026 rate, per ETH Zurich Staffnet 'Fixed-rate salaries' page (https://ethz.ch/staffnet/en/employment-and-work/employment/salary/fixed-rate-salaries.html) and ETH Zurich 'Salary' welcome-center page (https://ethz.ch/en/the-eth-zurich/working-teaching-and-research/welcome-center/employment-contract-and-salary/salary.html), corroborated by a Dec 2025 ETH Staffnet news post announcing a Jan 2026 increase to the standard and rate-2 doctoral salary levels (https://ethz.ch/staffnet/en/news-and-events/internal-news/archive/2025/12/increase-in-salary-rates-for-doctoral-students-standard-and-rate-2.html). The CHF 61,300/64,200 year-1/year-4 figures were confirmed via web search summary of the official salary table (rendered as an image on ETH's page, not machine-extractable) — cross-checked against two independent search results. ETH doctoral students are hired at 'standard rate' or a higher rate (2-5) depending on funding source/supervisor discretion; standard rate is the floor mandated for all PhD students since July 2020. Net-pay estimate is a rough calculation, not an official figure — actual net depends on canton of residence, religion (church tax), marital status, and pension contributions.
EPFL — Doctoral assistant (PhD), gross annual starting salaryCHF 55,225/year gross (approx. CHF 4,270-4,600/month depending on whether divided over 12 or ~13 payments), increasing by about CHF 1,000/year in subsequent years, subject to budget. Est. net (single, Lausanne/Vaud canton, foreign national on withholding tax) ≈ CHF 3,800-4,100/month net (ESTIMATE).Effective 1 Jan 2026 figure, per official EPFL page 'Doctoral Students' salary' (https://www.epfl.ch/education/phd/doctoral-studies-structure/doctoral-students-salary/): 'The basic starting salary of an EPFL doctoral assistant is CHF 55'225.- gross per annum,' with 'an increment of CHF 1,000 per year... provided that budgetary resources and economic conditions allow.' This reflects a unified EPFL-wide PhD salary structure (single rate, no min/max range) introduced 1 May 2024 and adjusted for 2026, following the SNSF's minimum-salary increases for doctoral students (see EPFL Actu article: https://actu.epfl.ch/news/snsf-is-increasing-the-minimum-salaries-for-doctor/). Net-pay estimate is rough, not sourced from an official calculator — Vaud canton withholding tax + AHV/ALV/pension deductions for a single person typically run about 13-16% at this income level.
Karolinska Institutet (Sweden) — Doctoral studentship (anställning som doktorand), gross monthly salary ladder, without medical degreeSEK 32,100/month (months 1-12) -> SEK 32,600/month (months 13-24) -> SEK 34,300/month (months 25-36) -> SEK 34,700/month (from month 37) -> SEK 36,000/month after PhD completion (+ one-off SEK 3,000 payment). Net pay is explicitly given by KI's own HR document for scholarship-equivalent comparison: net ≈ SEK 26,185 (yr1) / 26,544 (yr2) / 27,866 (yr3) / 28,172 (yr4), i.e. roughly SEK 26,200-28,200/month net for a standard 4-year, 100%, non-medical PhD.Valid from 1 October 2025, per KI's official 'Quick Reference Guide - Doctoral students' (Human Resources Department, latest update 2025-11-12), obtained directly from https://staff.ki.se/media/568/download, linked from KI Staff Portal 'Employment as a doctoral student at KI' (https://staff.ki.se/doctoral-education/employment-as-a-doctoral-student-at-ki). Figures are gross ('Without medical degree' column) and the KI document itself computes net by subtracting tax per Solna municipality 2025 tax table 29 column 1 (this is KI's own official net calculation, not an external estimate — used by KI to benchmark external scholarship top-ups against actual employed-doctoral-student net income). If on a part-time (50%) 8-year track the steps double in duration (increases after 24/48/72 months instead of 12/24/36); a 5-year plan with 20% teaching duties shifts steps to 15/30/45 months. This is Sweden's normal route (doktorandanställning = actual employment, not a 'stipend' in the fellowship sense) and is the standard, most common funding mechanism for PhDs at KI.

Cost of living (monthly)

CityEstimateNotes
Oxford, UK£1,400–2,100/month (~£1,750 mid-estimate)Found (official). University of Oxford living-cost estimates for 2025-26 (£1,425–2,035/mo) and 2026-27 (£1,405–2,105/mo), single grad student, no dependents, incl. rent/utilities (~£900-1,150 shared housing or £790-990 college accommodation) + food (£330-515). Source: ox.ac.uk/students/fees-funding/living-costs (University of Oxford, accessed 2026).
Cambridge, UK~£1,305/month (£11,745/yr term-time; £15,660/yr if in Cambridge year-round)Found (official). University of Cambridge Postgraduate Study official living-cost estimate for 2026-27, calculated May 2025 using Bank of England inflation forecasts. Source: postgraduate.study.cam.ac.uk/finance/maintenance (University of Cambridge, accessed 2026). Estimate may run low vs. actual market rent given persistent UK rent inflation.
London, UK£1,300–2,750/month (~£1,900-2,000 realistic for student incl. shared-flat rent)Found (aggregated). UniversityLiving/UCAS-sourced range for London students 2025-26/2026-27. Save The Student 2026 Accommodation Survey: avg rent alone £793-1,800/mo depending on zone/type; food ~£155-200/mo; transport £90-130/mo. UCL halls £154-456/week. Source: universityliving.com/blog/student-finances/cost-of-living-in-london (accessed 2026), UCL official cost page ucl.ac.uk/scholarships/how-much-does-it-cost-study-ucl. Wide range reflects zone/accommodation-type variance — treat £1,900-2,200 as a more realistic PhD-student central estimate (estimate, not directly sourced).
Boston / Cambridge, MA, USA~$2,500–3,800/month total (rent $1,075-3,701 on-campus grad housing or ~$2,162 avg 1BR off-campus in Cambridge, MA, + ~$1,476/month non-rent costs per Numbeo Boston)Found (mixed official + Numbeo). MIT Office of Graduate Education on-campus housing range and off-campus 1BR average (facts as of 2025-26, rates +3.5% for AY25-26); non-rent living costs (food/transport/utilities/misc) from Numbeo Boston, June 2026 snapshot ($1,476/mo single person excl. rent). Sources: oge.mit.edu/graduate-admissions/costs-funding/cost-of-attendance, graduatehousing.mit.edu/graduate-housing-rate-setting, numbeo.com/cost-of-living/in/Boston (accessed 2026). Combined total is an estimate synthesized from these two partial sources, not a single authoritative figure.
Palo Alto / Stanford, CA, USA~$3,300–4,500/month (housing $1,100-3,000 + living expenses; PhD stipend ~$3,750-4,580/mo i.e. $45k-55k/yr)Found (mixed). Stanford Financial Aid grad budget page + first-hand PhD money-diary account (living on $3,950/mo total in Palo Alto) + housing range $1,100 (shared room) to $2,600-3,000 (studio). Palo Alto cost of living is 49% above US national average (source cited within search aggregation, likely derived from Numbeo/PayScale). Sources: financialaid.stanford.edu/grad/budget, convexoptimist.substack.com/p/phd-money-diaries-living-on-3950 (accessed 2026). Total figure is an estimate combining housing + stipend data, not one official all-in number.
Princeton, NJ, USA~$3,218/month ($38,610/yr official grad living-expense estimate, 2025-26)Found (official). Princeton Graduate School's official estimated annual living expense for a single graduate student, 2025-26, incl. 3.2% avg stipend/fellowship increase for the year. On-campus housing $1,049-4,644/mo; off-campus 1BR avg ~$2,500/mo in Princeton itself, ~$1,900/mo in nearby Plainsboro. Source: gradschool.princeton.edu/financial-support/financial-support-model/funding-calendar-university-rates-costs (Princeton University, accessed 2026).
Pasadena / Caltech, CA, USA~$2,600–3,600/month (single grad apartment ~$1,370/mo + board ~$806/mo + personal ~$401/mo, inflated from AY2022-23 official figures; current 1BR market rent ~$2,366-2,400/mo per 2025 data)Found but partly dated. Most detailed official Caltech Graduate Studies Office breakdown available in search was AY2022-23 (room/board/personal expense components); current page exists at gradoffice.caltech.edu/financialsupport/budget but exact updated monthly figures weren't retrievable via search snippet. Current market rent for 1BR Pasadena ~$2,366 (Aug 2025) to $2,400 (2025) per rental-market sources (RentCafe/LA Metro Home Finder). Caltech grad stipend for AY2025-26 is $4,020/mo ($48,244/yr), rising to $4,166/mo ($50,000/yr) Sept 2026 — treat as rough guide since it mixes 2022-23 baseline + 2025 market rent; recommend checking gradoffice.caltech.edu/financialsupport/budget directly for the current official figure.
Zurich, Switzerland (ETH Zurich)CHF 2,000–2,500/month (CHF 1,700-2,100 if in dormitory)Found (official). ETH Zurich's own cost-of-living estimate, recommending CHF 20,000-26,000/year. Breakdown: rent CHF 800-1,000/mo average (dorm CHF 400-900, WG/flatshare CHF 700-1,500, private studio CHF 1,500-2,200); health insurance CHF 100-250/mo; transport ~CHF 95/mo. Source: ethz.ch/content/dam/ethz/main/education/finanzielles/files-en/cost-of-living.pdf and ethz.ch/en/the-eth-zurich/.../cost-of-living.html (ETH Zurich, accessed 2026).
Lausanne, Switzerland (EPFL)CHF 1,500–2,500/month (~CHF 2,500/mo per EPFL's own CHF 30,000/yr recommendation)Found (official). EPFL's official recommendation is to budget ~CHF 30,000/year (~CHF 2,500/month) for living expenses. University residence rooms CHF 500-750/mo vs. free-market room CHF 800-1,200/mo. Note: starting Autumn 2025, EPFL (and ETH) tripled tuition for non-Swiss/non-EU students from CHF 730 to CHF 2,190/semester — Swiss/EU students unaffected; as an EU applicant this does not apply to you. Source: epfl.ch/education/studies/en/financing-study and epfl.ch/education/studies/en/preparation-epfl/manage-budget (EPFL, accessed 2026).
Stockholm, Sweden (Karolinska Institutet)SEK 11,750–15,000/month (typical breakdown: food SEK 2,700 + accommodation SEK 6,000 + transport SEK 400 + phone/internet SEK 400 + misc SEK 1,200)Found (official + aggregated). Karolinska Institutet's own minimum guidance is SEK 11,750/month; other sources cite SEK 12,000-15,000/month as a more realistic Stockholm budget. Sweden's 2026 residence-permit minimum requirement is SEK 10,656/month (floor, not a realistic living budget). Accommodation ranges SEK 3,500 (student housing) to SEK 9,000+ (private 1BR city centre). Note: Karolinska PhD positions are normally salaried employee positions (not stipends) under Swedish law, and KI does not separately fund living costs beyond the PhD salary. Source: education.ki.se/living-expenses-and-funding (Karolinska Institutet, accessed 2026).

Effort per task

TaskHours
Research proposal (core, reusable base draft: literature scan, framing, aims, methods, feasibility)40-70 (ESTIMATE — verify)
Research proposal — per-scheme/per-supervisor tailoring after base draft exists5-10 each (ESTIMATE)
Personal statement / statement of purpose (core reusable draft + iterations)15-25 (ESTIMATE)
Personal statement — per-program tailoring2-4 each (ESTIMATE)
Gates Cambridge extra essays (leadership/commitment to improving lives, ~2 essays)10-16 (ESTIMATE)
Knight-Hennessy extra essays + video (leadership, personal, short-answers)12-20 (ESTIMATE)
Other scheme-specific extra essays (Clarendon/Harding/Rhodes-style, each)4-8 each (ESTIMATE)
Supervisor outreach email set (identify targets, read papers, draft + personalize per PI, follow-ups)1.5-3 per PI; 30-50 total for ~20 PIs (ESTIMATE)
Reference wrangling (identify/ask referees, brief them, provide packet, chase deadlines)8-15 (ESTIMATE)
Transcript / document prep (official transcripts, translations, certified copies, CV, degree certs)8-15 (ESTIMATE)
English test prep (IELTS/TOEFL study + booking + sitting) — lower if already fluent15-40 (ESTIMATE — highly variable)
Application form filling + portal admin per program (fields, uploads, review, submit)2-4 each (ESTIMATE)
TOTAL realistic effort for a full UK+US cycle (~8-12 targets, shared core assets)250-400 (ESTIMATE — verify against your own writing speed)
